findings.title: Bare catch block silently swallows all errors in tick()
findings.description: The catch block in tick() has no error parameter binding -- the error object is completely discarded. No logging, no emission, no observability. Errors from screenshot capture, pHash computation, and VLM processing are all silently swallowed. The return value `false` is indistinguishable from a legitimate dedup skip, making it impossible for callers to differentiate between 'screenshot was similar' and 'VLM provider crashed'. The comment acknowledges logging is needed but defers it. This violates multiple CLAUDE.md forbidden patterns: `catch (e) {}` (silent swallow hides bugs) and `catch (e) { return null }` (converts error to invalid state).
findings.suggestion: Accept a structured logger (e.g., @guiiai/logg) via ScreenshotPipelineOptions and add an onError callback. Bind the error parameter. Log at WARN level with context (which stage failed, timestamp). Return a discriminated union: `Promise<{ status: 'processed', context: ProcessedScreenshotContext } | { status: 'skipped' } | { status: 'error', error: Error }>` instead of a boolean, so callers can observe failures and distinguish dedup from error.

findings.title: Silent catch in tick() has no test and swallows all errors
findings.description: There is no test in screenshot-pipeline.test.ts that verifies error behavior: what happens when screenshotProvider.capture() throws, when computePHash throws on corrupt data, or when vlmProvider.describeImage() throws. A caller receiving false cannot distinguish 'deduped' from 'crashed'. Each failure mode should have explicit test coverage.
findings.suggestion: Add tests for each failure mode: (a) screenshotProvider.capture() rejects, (b) corrupt image causes computePHash to fail, (c) vlmProvider.describeImage() rejects. Verify tick() returns the expected result (or a typed result that distinguishes dedup from error). Consider returning a discriminated union like { status: 'processed' | 'deduped' | 'error', error?: Error }.

findings.title: Comment says 'log and continue' but code silently swallows errors
findings.description: The comment states 'log and continue on next tick' but the catch block does not log anything -- it silently swallows the error and returns false. A future maintainer reading this comment would believe errors are being logged somewhere. The second line 'In production, this would use a structured logger' is a disguised TODO, which is a forbidden pattern (code should be complete or not committed).
findings.suggestion: Either add actual logging (e.g., using @guiiai/logg structured logger) and keep the comment, or change the comment to accurately describe the current behavior. Better yet, inject a logger and actually log the error with context. Remove the aspirational 'In production...' comment -- either implement it now or track it in the task system.

findings.title: ScreenshotPipeline exported but not consumed by any entry point
findings.code_snippet:
export { ScreenshotPipeline } from './capture/screenshot-pipeline'
export type { ScreenshotPipelineOptions } from './capture/screenshot-pipeline'
findings.description: ScreenshotPipeline, ScreenshotProcessor, VlmProvider, and ProcessedScreenshotContext are exported from the package but have zero consumers outside of test files. The existing app integration at apps/stage-tamagotchi/src/main/services/anima/context-engine.ts still only uses ScreenshotCapture directly -- it was not updated to use the new pipeline. Grep across the entire repo confirms no import of ScreenshotPipeline from @proj-airi/context-engine in any app or service. Per CLAUDE.md integration rules: 'Code written but not reachable from any entry point is dead-on-arrival.' This is a horizontal-only change that adds library code without wiring it to any live path.
findings.suggestion: Update apps/stage-tamagotchi/src/main/services/anima/context-engine.ts (or the appropriate entry point) to instantiate ScreenshotPipeline with an ElectronScreenshotProvider and a concrete VlmProvider (e.g., xsAI-based). Wire start()/stop() to the app lifecycle. This proves the vertical slice: Electron capture -> pHash dedup -> VLM understanding -> context emission.

findings.title: ScreenshotProcessor error wrapping test does not verify cause chain
findings.description: The error wrapping test verifies the outer error message but does not verify the error cause chain. The ScreenshotProcessor uses `new Error('Screenshot processing failed', { cause: error })` which preserves the original error. The test should verify that error.cause contains the original 'API rate limit exceeded' error. Without this assertion, a regression that drops the cause would not be caught.
findings.suggestion: Add cause chain verification: `await expect(processor.process({ buffer: Buffer.from('test'), timestamp: Date.now() })).rejects.toSatisfy((error: Error) => { return error.message === 'Screenshot processing failed' && error.cause instanceof Error && error.cause.message === 'API rate limit exceeded' })`

findings.title: Test helper JSDoc incorrectly claims different colors produce different hashes
findings.code_snippet:
/**
 * Create a solid-color PNG buffer for testing.
 * Different colors produce visually different images -> different hashes.
 */
findings.description: The JSDoc states 'Different colors produce visually different images -> different hashes' but this is factually wrong for perceptual hashing. The same test file explicitly acknowledges at line 59 that 'Solid-color images all produce the same pHash (all pixels = mean -> all 1).' This comment directly contradicts the known behavior documented elsewhere in the same file and will mislead anyone who tries to use this helper for hash-differentiation tests.
findings.suggestion: Fix the JSDoc to accurately describe the helper's behavior and limitations: '/** Create a solid-color PNG buffer for testing. Note: all solid-color images produce the same pHash (all pixels equal mean -> all bits 1). Use images with different spatial patterns to get distinct hashes. */'

findings.title: lastHash dedup state stored only in memory, not persisted
findings.code_snippet: private lastHash: string | null = null
findings.description: The pipeline's dedup state (lastHash) is a closure-scoped mutable variable that lives only in memory. If the Electron main process restarts (crash, update, OS restart), the hash is lost and the next capture will always be treated as new, sending a potentially identical screenshot through the VLM again (wasting API cost). Per CLAUDE.md architecture rule: 'Business state in memory' is a forbidden pattern -- critical state must be persistable/recoverable/observable.
findings.suggestion: Accept an optional persistence adapter (e.g., { loadLastHash(): Promise<string|null>, saveLastHash(hash: string): Promise<void> }) in ScreenshotPipelineOptions. On tick(), save the hash after processing. On start(), load the previous hash. For a walking skeleton this can be a simple file or SQLite row (the package already depends on better-sqlite3).

findings.title: Fire-and-forget async calls to tick() in start()
findings.description: The `void this.tick()` calls are fire-and-forget async invocations. While tick() internally catches all errors (preventing unhandled promise rejections), the combination of fire-and-forget + catch-all-swallow creates a completely opaque system: errors happen, nobody knows, nobody can react. If the first tick() fails (e.g., provider is misconfigured), the pipeline silently starts in an error state.
findings.suggestion: For the initial tick in start(), consider awaiting or chaining: `this.tick().catch(err => this.onError?.(err))`. For interval ticks, use the same onError callback pattern. Alternatively, make start() async and await the first tick to surface immediate configuration errors.

findings.title: computePHash accepts any Buffer with no validation
findings.description: computePHash is a public API that accepts any Buffer and passes it directly to sharp. An empty buffer (Buffer.alloc(0)) -- which is what Electron desktopCapturer returns on permission failure -- will cause sharp to throw an opaque native error. Since this is a boundary function (accepts external data from the screenshot provider), input validation is required.
findings.suggestion: Add a guard at the top of computePHash: if (!imageBuffer || imageBuffer.length === 0) throw new Error('computePHash: imageBuffer is empty or undefined'). This provides a clear, debuggable error message at the boundary rather than a cryptic sharp native crash.

findings.title: VlmResult.activity typed as bare string instead of union
findings.code_snippet: activity: string
findings.description: The `activity` field on VlmResult, ProcessedScreenshotContext, and ScreenshotUnderstanding is documented as a category with known values ('coding', 'browsing', 'writing') but typed as bare `string`. This is primitive obsession -- the type system cannot prevent invalid activity strings from flowing through the pipeline.
findings.suggestion: Define a union type: `export type ActivityCategory = 'coding' | 'browsing' | 'writing' | 'reading' | 'communicating' | 'other'` and use it for the `activity` field across all types. If the VLM can return arbitrary categories, use a branded string or validate at the VLM boundary.

findings.title: Near-duplicate split-image pixel buffer generation across test files
findings.description: The horizontal-split and vertical-split image generation pattern appears in both phash.test.ts (lines 65-88) and screenshot-pipeline.test.ts (lines 17-50). Both create the same pair of images with ~30 lines of nearly identical code. This is a maintenance burden -- if the image generation logic needs to change, both files must be updated in lockstep.
findings.suggestion: Extract shared test fixture to __tests__/fixtures/test-images.ts with `createHorizontalSplitPng(width, height)` and `createVerticalSplitPng(width, height)` functions. This eliminates ~30 lines of duplication and creates a single source of truth.

findings.title: StubVlmProvider duplicated across two test files
findings.description: StubVlmProvider is defined in both screenshot-processor.test.ts and screenshot-pipeline.test.ts with slightly different implementations. Both serve the same purpose: stubbing the external VLM API boundary. Having two separate stubs means two places to update if VlmProvider changes.
findings.suggestion: Merge both variants into a single configurable stub in __tests__/fixtures/stub-vlm-provider.ts that supports both custom result and call counting. One place to update when VlmProvider interface evolves.

findings.title: ProcessedScreenshotContext.hash is bare string, not branded
findings.code_snippet: hash: string
findings.description: The `hash` field represents a 64-character binary string (pHash output) but is typed as bare `string`. Any string is a valid hash value as far as the type system is concerned. A type alias or branded type would make the domain intent explicit and prevent accidental misuse.
findings.suggestion: Define a type alias or branded type: `export type PHash = string & { readonly __brand: 'PHash' }` and use it as the return type of computePHash() and in ProcessedScreenshotContext.hash. At minimum create a type alias: `export type PHash = string`.

findings.title: computePHash not tested with invalid/corrupt image buffer
findings.description: The phash.test.ts tests cover valid images of various sizes and colors, but there is no test for invalid input: an empty buffer (Buffer.alloc(0)), a non-image buffer (Buffer.from('not-an-image')), or a truncated/corrupt PNG. The function has no try/catch, so sharp errors propagate uncaught -- the test suite should document and verify this error behavior.
findings.suggestion: Add boundary tests: (1) computePHash(Buffer.alloc(0)) should reject/throw with a meaningful error. (2) computePHash(Buffer.from('not-an-image')) should reject/throw. These tests document the error contract for callers.

findings.title: onContext callback errors silently swallowed by catch-all
findings.code_snippet: this.onContext?.(context)
findings.description: The `onContext` callback is invoked inside the try block (line 89). If the consumer's callback throws an exception, the error propagates to the catch block on line 92 and is silently swallowed. This means bugs in the callback consumer are hidden -- the pipeline reports false (as if deduped) and the consumer never learns their handler threw.
findings.suggestion: Either move the onContext call outside the try/catch so callback errors propagate to the caller, or wrap it in its own try/catch with explicit error reporting. Option 1 is preferred -- the pipeline should not be responsible for swallowing consumer errors.

findings.title: Concurrent tick() calls can cause race in dedup state
findings.description: tick() is async but start() calls it via setInterval with void this.tick(). If a tick takes longer than intervalMs, two ticks run concurrently. Both read the same this.lastHash, both may decide the screenshot is new, both update this.lastHash, and both call the VLM -- defeating the dedup purpose and doubling API costs.
findings.suggestion: Add a simple mutex/guard: 'private ticking = false'. At the top of tick(), if (this.ticking) return false; this.ticking = true; and in a finally block: this.ticking = false. This ensures at most one tick runs at a time.

findings.title: ScreenshotPipeline accepts unvalidated numeric options
findings.code_snippet:
constructor(options: ScreenshotPipelineOptions) {
    this.capture = new ScreenshotCapture(options.screenshotProvider)
    this.processor = new ScreenshotProcessor(options.vlmProvider)
    this.intervalMs = options.intervalMs ?? 60_000
    this.similarityThreshold = options.similarityThreshold ?? 5
    this.onContext = options.onContext
  }
findings.description: The ScreenshotPipeline constructor applies defaults for intervalMs and similarityThreshold but does not validate that the resulting values are positive. An intervalMs of 0 would create a tight loop; a negative similarityThreshold would never match.
findings.suggestion: Add validation in the constructor: `if (this.intervalMs <= 0) throw new Error('intervalMs must be positive')` and `if (this.similarityThreshold < 0) throw new Error('similarityThreshold must be non-negative')`.

findings.title: hammingDistance not tested with empty strings
findings.description: The hammingDistance tests cover identical, single-bit-different, completely opposite, and length-mismatch cases. However, the empty string boundary is not tested: hammingDistance('', '') should return 0. Also, the error test does not verify the error message content.
findings.suggestion: Add: (1) expect(hammingDistance('', '')).toBe(0) to document the empty-string contract. (2) Update the throw test to verify the error message: expect(() => hammingDistance('01', '011')).toThrow('Hash length mismatch: 2 vs 3').

findings.title: No VlmProvider implementation exists anywhere in the codebase
findings.description: VlmProvider is an interface with no concrete implementation in the codebase (only test stubs). Without a real implementation, the ScreenshotPipeline cannot be instantiated in any app. The walking skeleton principle requires that the first deliverable be a minimal end-to-end flow returning real data, not just an interface.
findings.suggestion: Create a concrete VlmProvider implementation (e.g., XsaiVlmProvider) that wraps xsAI's vision model API. Even a minimal implementation that sends a base64 image and returns the parsed response would complete the vertical slice.

findings.title: Error messages lack input context for debugging
findings.code_snippet: throw new Error('Screenshot processing failed', { cause: error })
findings.description: The error message 'Screenshot processing failed' in ScreenshotProcessor.process() lacks context about what was being processed. While { cause: error } preserves the error chain, the wrapping message provides no information about buffer size, provider, or correlation ID. When multiple screenshots fail, these errors are hard to correlate.
findings.suggestion: Include relevant context: `throw new Error(`Screenshot processing failed (buffer=${screenshot.buffer.length} bytes, ts=${screenshot.timestamp})`, { cause: error })`.

findings.title: Magic number 5 for default pHash similarity threshold
findings.code_snippet: threshold = 5,
findings.description: The default similarity threshold of 5 appears as a magic number in areSimilar() and is repeated as the default in ScreenshotPipelineOptions. While HASH_SIZE is properly named, the threshold default is not, creating duplication risk.
findings.suggestion: Extract to named constant: `export const DEFAULT_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD = 5` and import it in both phash.ts and screenshot-pipeline.ts.

findings.title: ScreenshotUnderstanding duplicates ProcessedScreenshotContext fields
findings.description: ScreenshotUnderstanding has the exact same fields as ProcessedScreenshotContext minus `hash`. This creates a maintenance burden -- if a field is added to ProcessedScreenshotContext, ScreenshotUnderstanding must be updated manually.
findings.suggestion: Replace with: `export type ScreenshotUnderstanding = Omit<ProcessedScreenshotContext, 'hash'>`. This keeps both types in sync.

positive_observations: Clean separation of concerns: phash (pure function), ScreenshotProcessor (single responsibility VLM wrapper), and ScreenshotPipeline (orchestrator) follow Functional Core / Imperative Shell architecture well
Well-defined TypeScript interfaces (ScreenshotProvider, VlmProvider) enable dependency inversion at boundaries
Test doubles in tests correctly use '// Test Double rationale:' comments per CLAUDE.md external API exception, implementing the actual interface rather than jest.fn()
pHash tests are exemplary Functional Core unit tests: real sharp library for image creation, pure input/output verification, no mocks whatsoever
Error wrapping in ScreenshotProcessor.process() includes cause chain for debuggability
Pipeline tests exercise the real dedup logic through actual pHash computation (not mocked), testing the collaboration between capture -> phash -> processor
All source files are well within size limits (largest is 109 lines). Functions are short with clear single responsibilities. Maximum nesting depth is 2 levels.
Guard clauses and early returns used consistently (start() returns early if timer exists, hammingDistance throws early on length mismatch)
Pure functions in phash.ts (computePHash, hammingDistance, areSimilar) correctly belong to the Functional Core with no class wrappers
Named constant HASH_SIZE = 8 demonstrates good naming practice over magic numbers
ScreenshotPipeline has good encapsulation with all fields private and controlled mutation through methods (start/stop/tick/resetDedup)
JSDoc comments on all exported interfaces and functions follow project conventions consistently
Algorithm description in computePHash JSDoc accurately matches the 3-step implementation (resize, mean, threshold)
Interface JSDoc comments in types.ts explain 'why' rather than just restating type signatures
ScreenshotCapture.capture() and ScreenshotProcessor.process() both use { cause: error } for error chaining, preserving the original error stack trace
